title: Chemical Bonding & Molecular Structure Test - 2 (Topic Test)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: What is the total resistance in the following circuit element?
questions.solution:
To find the total resistance in the given circuit, we first need to understand the configuration of the resistors. The image shows three resistors arranged in a combination of series and parallel.

Let's denote the resistances as follows:

  • Two resistors in series with each resistance $$R$$
  • One resistor in parallel with the series combination mentioned above with resistance $$R$$

First, we calculate the equivalent resistance of the two resistors in series:

$$R_{series} = R + R = 2R$$

Next, we consider the parallel combination of this series resistance with the third resistor:

The total resistance for resistors in parallel, where one resistor has resistance $$R$$ and the other has resistance $$2R$$, is given by the formula:

$$\frac{1}{R_{total}} = \frac{1}{R} + \frac{1}{2R}$$

Combining the two fractions, we get:

$$\frac{1}{R_{total}} = \frac{2}{2R} + \frac{1}{2R} = \frac{3}{2R}$$

Taking the reciprocal to find the total resistance:

$$R_{total} = \frac{2R}{3}$$

Therefore, the total resistance in the circuit is $$\frac{2R}{3}$$.

So the correct option is:

Option D $$\frac{2R}{3}$$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements is NOT correct?
options: An ammeter is always connected in series in the circuit to measure the current
A Voltmeter is always connected to parallel in a circuit to measure the voltage
A voltmeter has a high resistance and an ammeter has a low resistance
A voltmeter has a low resistance and an ammeter has a high resistance
questions.solution:
The incorrect statement is Option 4.

Explanation:

An ammeter must have a low internal resistance and be connected in series so it doesn’t appreciably change the circuit current.

A voltmeter must have a high internal resistance and be connected in parallel so it doesn’t appreciably draw current.

Option 4 reverses these resistances and is therefore not correct.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: A current of 0.6 A is drawn by an electric bulb for 10 minutes. Which one of the following is the amount of electric charge that flows through the circuit?
questions.solution:
To determine the amount of electric charge that flows through the circuit, we can use the relationship between current, charge, and time. The equation we will use is:

$$Q = I \cdot t$$

where:

  • $ Q $ is the electric charge in coulombs (C).
  • $ I $ is the current in amperes (A).
  • $ t $ is the time in seconds (s).

Given:

  • Current $ I = 0.6 $ A
  • Time $ t = 10 $ minutes

First, we need to convert the time from minutes to seconds:

$$ t = 10 \text{ minutes} \times 60 \text{ seconds/minute} = 600 \text{ seconds} $$

Now we can substitute $ I $ and $ t $ into the equation:

$$ Q = 0.6 \text{ A} \times 600 \text{ s} = 360 \text{ C} $$

Therefore, the amount of electric charge that flows through the circuit is 360 C. Thus, the correct option is:

Option C: 360 C

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements about Light year:

1. Light year is a unit for measurement of very large distances.

2. Light year is a unit for measurement of very large time intervals.

3. Light year is a unit for measurement of intensity of light.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Only statement 1 is correct.

A light year is a unit of distance, not time or intensity of light. It is defined as the distance that light travels in a vacuum in one year. The speed of light is approximately $299,792,458$ meters per second, so over the course of a year, this distance is about:

$$ 1 \text{ light year} = 9.461 \times 10^{15} \text{ meters} $$

Thus, the correct option is D: 1 only.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: At uniform speed the acceleration is
questions.solution:
Option 3: Zero

Since acceleration is the rate of change of velocity, for uniform speed (and straight-line motion) we have

$$a = \frac{dv}{dt} = 0\,. $$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The flash of lightning is seen before the thunderstorm is heard. It verifies that
options: sound travels much faster than light
light travels much faster than sound
light and sound both travel with same speed
intensity of flash of lightning is very high during thunderstorm
questions.solution:
The phenomenon where the flash of lightning is seen before the thunderstorm is heard can be explained by the difference in the speeds at which light and sound travel. Light travels significantly faster than sound. Thus, the correct option is:

Option B: light travels much faster than sound

To elaborate, light travels at an approximate speed of $$3 \times 10^8 \, \text{m/s}$$ in a vacuum, and slightly slower in the atmosphere. On the other hand, sound travels at a much slower speed of approximately $$343 \, \text{m/s}$$ in air at room temperature. Because of this large discrepancy in speeds, the light from the lightning is observed almost instantaneously, while the sound waves take a longer time to reach the observer's ears. This delay is why we see the flash of lightning before we hear the thunder.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The image we see in plane mirror is
options: real and thus can be photographed.
virtual and nearer than the object.
questions.solution:
The image observed in a plane mirror is described as virtual and is laterally inverted. Therefore, the correct option is:

Option C

Virtual and is laterally inverted.

Explanation:

Virtual Image: A virtual image is formed when the outgoing rays from a point of the object appear to diverge. In the case of a plane mirror, the image cannot be projected onto a screen because the light rays do not actually converge at the image location; they only appear to do so when seen by the observer.

Lateral Inversion: This term refers to the horizontal reversal of the image. For example, if the left hand of a person is raised while standing in front of the mirror, it appears that the right hand is raised in the mirror image.

Virtual images cannot be captured directly on a screen but can be seen by looking into the mirror. It is important to note that a photograph of the mirror itself capturing the virtual image can be taken, but it would not be a direct image of the virtual image.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following does not apply to sound waves in fluids?
options: They travel faster in liquids than in gases
questions.solution:
The option that does not apply to sound waves in fluids is:

Option C

They are transverse

Explanation:

Sound waves in fluids (liquids and gases) are longitudinal waves, not transverse waves. In longitudinal waves, the oscillations of the particles of the medium are parallel to the direction of wave propagation. This means that particles in the medium move back and forth in the same direction that the wave is traveling.

Let's break down the other options to see why they do apply to sound waves in fluids:

Option A: They transport energy

Sound waves indeed transport energy. As a sound wave propagates through a medium, it transfers energy from one particle to another through the medium.

Option B: They need a medium to travel

Sound waves require a medium (solid, liquid, or gas) to travel. They cannot propagate in a vacuum because there are no particles to transmit the vibrations.

Option D: They travel faster in liquids than in gases

Sound waves generally travel faster in liquids than in gases. This is because particles in liquids are more closely packed than in gases, allowing the sound waves to transmit vibrations more efficiently.

Therefore, Option C is the correct answer since sound waves in fluids are not transverse waves.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: A block of mass 2 kg, moving with the initial speed of 3 m/s comes to rest on a rough horizontal surface after travelling a distance of 3 m. The magnitude of the frictional force is :
questions.solution:
To find the frictional force acting on the block, we can use the work-energy principle. The principle states that the work done by all forces acting on an object is equal to the change in the object's kinetic energy.

First, let's denote the mass of the block as $ m $, the initial speed as $ v_i $, the final speed $ v_f $, the distance traveled $ d $, and the frictional force $ f_{fric} $.

Given :

  • Mass, $ m = 2 \, \text{kg} $
  • Initial speed, $ v_i = 3 \, \text{m/s} $
  • Final speed, $ v_f = 0 \, \text{m/s} $ (since the block comes to rest)
  • Distance traveled, $ d = 3 \, \text{m} $

The initial kinetic energy of the block is given by :

$$ KE_i = \frac{1}{2} m v_i^2 $$

Substituting the values :

$$ KE_i = \frac{1}{2} \times 2 \, \text{kg} \times (3 \, \text{m/s})^2 = \frac{1}{2} \times 2 \times 9 = 9 \, \text{J} $$

The block comes to rest, so its final kinetic energy is zero :

$$ KE_f = 0 $$

The work done by the frictional force is equal to the change in kinetic energy of the block :

$$ W = KE_f - KE_i = 0 - 9 \, \text{J} = -9 \, \text{J} $$

The work done by the frictional force can also be calculated using :

$$ W = f_{fric} \times d $$

We know the distance $ d $ is 3 meters, so :

$$ -9 \, \text{J} = f_{fric} \times 3 \, \text{m} $$

Solving for the frictional force :

$$ f_{fric} = \frac{-9 \, \text{J}}{3 \, \text{m}} = -3 \, \text{N} $$

The magnitude of the frictional force is 3 N (the negative sign indicates the direction opposite to the motion). Therefore, the correct answer is :

Option B : 3 N

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following scientists mentioned that an electron in an atom could revolve in certain stable orbits without the emission of radiant energy?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option C: Niels Bohr.

Niels Bohr was a pioneering physicist who made fundamental contributions to our understanding of atomic structure and quantum mechanics. In 1913, Bohr introduced his model of the atom, which incorporated some aspects of classical physics and early quantum theories. One of the key postulates of the Bohr model is that electrons can revolve around the nucleus of an atom in specific, quantized orbits without the emission of radiant energy. These stable orbits correspond to certain fixed energy levels.

Bohr proposed that while the electron remains in one of these discrete energy levels, no energy is radiated. Radiant energy is emitted or absorbed only when an electron transitions from one energy level to another. This idea was revolutionary because, according to classical electromagnetic theory, an accelerating charge such as an electron in orbit should continuously emit radiation and lose energy, leading to a collapse of the orbit. Bohr's model provided a way to circumvent this problem by introducing quantum concepts.

Bohr's theory can be summarized by the equation for the quantized angular momentum of an electron:

$$L = n \hbar$$

where:

  • $$L$$ is the angular momentum
  • $$n$$ is a positive integer (quantum number)
  • $$\hbar$$ is the reduced Planck's constant ( $$\hbar = \frac{h}{2 \pi}$$ )

This discovery was critical in the development of quantum mechanics and helped to explain phenomena that could not be accounted for by classical physics alone. It laid the groundwork for future atomic models and a more comprehensive understanding of atomic and subatomic processes.

In conclusion, Niels Bohr's concept of electrons revolving in quantized stable orbits without radiating energy was fundamental in explaining atomic stability and the discrete nature of atomic spectra.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText:
Which of the following statements correctly explains/explain the existence of a positive force between two electric charges?

1. Both the charges are positive.

2. Both the charges are negative.

3. Both the charges are oppositely charged.

Select the correct answer using the code given below.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText:
The electrostatic potential doesn't depend on which of the following 

(1) It depends on the charge

(2) It depends on the endpoints of the path through work done

(3) It depends on the path taken for the work done 

(4) It depends on the amount of work done
questions.solution:
The electrostatic potential does not depend on the path taken for the work done. This is because the electrostatic force is a conservative force, which means that the work done by or against an electrostatic force in moving a charge between two points depends only on the initial and final positions of the charge and is independent of the path taken.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option C: Only statement 3

questions.questionText: A mass M is dragged by a pulley on a horizontal plane by a force anti-parallel to its displacement. The work done in pulling the mass M is

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The composition of gases in exosphere is
questions.solution:
The exosphere is primarily composed of light gases. Helium and hydrogen are the two main constituents found in this outermost layer of Earth's atmosphere.

Therefore, the correct option is:

Option A: Helium and Hydrogen.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The heating element in an electric iron is usually made of
questions.solution:
The heating element in an electric iron is typically made of a material that has a high resistance to generate sufficient heat when electric current passes through it. Among the options provided, the best choice for this application is Nichrome, which is a nickel-chromium alloy. Nichrome is preferred because it has a high melting point, excellent resistance to oxidation, and stable resistance over a wide range of temperatures.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option C Nichrome

To elaborate more, when electric current flows through Nichrome, it encounters resistance, converting electrical energy into heat energy. This principle of Joule heating is utilized in various heating applications, such as in electric irons, toasters, and hair dryers. The equation governing this process is given by:

$$Q = I^2 R t$$

where:

  • $$Q$$ is the heat energy produced in joules
  • $$I$$ is the current in amperes
  • $$R$$ is the resistance in ohms
  • $$t$$ is the time in seconds

Using Nichrome allows manufacturers to create reliable and efficient heating elements that can withstand high temperatures and prolonged usage.
